name: BILL (Bill.com) AP/AR Automation API
what_it_does: BILL REST API for accounts payable and accounts receivable automation platform. Enables AI agents to manage invoice ingestion and processing workflows, handle vendor payment initiation and scheduling (ACH, check, wire, international), access approval workflow and authorization routing, retrieve payment status and reconciliation data, manage vendor and customer profile management, handle bill creation and approval automation, access cash flow forecasting and analytics, retrieve document management and receipt processing, manage multi-entity and subsidiary accounting, and integrate AP/AR data with QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, and Sage.
use_cases: Managing invoice processing and vendor payments from AP automation agents
Handling approval workflow routing from financial operations agents
Retrieving payment status and reconciliation from accounting automation agents
Integrating AP/AR data with accounting from SMB financial automation agents
not_for: Large enterprise ERP with built-in AP/AR without standalone automation needs
Personal finance without business invoice and vendor payment complexity
Real-time payment processing without invoice workflow context
best_when: A small-to-mid-size business using BILL wants AI agents to automate invoice processing, vendor payment scheduling, approval workflows, cash flow analysis, and accounting system integration.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: BILL manages real vendor payments — automated payment initiation without proper controls can result in misdirected payments or fraud. ACH and wire fraud are major risks in AP automation. Invoice automation must detect duplicate invoices and validate vendor banking details. Payment amounts must be verified against POs.

auth.notes: BILL uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. Organization and user-level credentials with payments, invoices, and approvals scopes. Developer portal with API documentation. Webhooks for payment and invoice events. Strong QuickBooks, Xero, and NetSuite integration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — AP/AR subscription required
pricing.notes: San Jose, California. NYSE: BILL. Founded 2006. SMB AP/AR automation leader. $1.2B+ annual revenue. 470,000+ businesses, 7M+ network members. $270B+ payment volume annually. BILL Spend & Expense (Divvy acquisition 2021) adds corporate cards. Competes with Tipalti and AvidXchange for AP automation.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; payment initiation rate limited

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Automated payment initiation — ACH and wire fraud prevention must validate vendor banking changes before payment
Duplicate invoice detection must precede automated processing — BILL does not automatically block duplicates
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API with developer portal
Payment approval workflows must not be bypassed — automated approval for high-value payments creates fraud risk
Multi-entity accounting requires explicit entity selection per API call — default entity may not match intended organization
Vendor banking detail changes should trigger re-verification workflow before next payment
agent_readiness.security_notes: AP/AR and payment data. SOC2, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. US data residency. Payment fraud prevention and vendor banking data security.

security_score_components.security_notes: AP/AR and payment automation. SOC2, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. US data residency. Payment fraud risk and vendor banking data.
